faq.question: Why does the log contain blocks of TypeScript code with `(unrecognised)` comments?
faq.answer:
The plugin is designed to monitor for features, options, or settings reported by the Home Connect API that are not yet known or documented. When an unknown key is encountered—such as `Dishcare.Dishwasher.Option.StorageFunction`—the plugin generates a TypeScript interface snippet in the logs to help identify the missing data. If you see these blocks in your log:

1. Ensure you are running the latest version of the plugin, as support for the specific value may have already been added.
2. If the message persists on the latest version, open a GitHub issue and provide the full log block.

This diagnostic output is a deliberate mechanism to identify undocumented appliance capabilities so they can be integrated into future releases of the plugin.
faq.semantic_abstract: The plugin performs runtime discovery of Home Connect API keys and identifies undocumented or unsupported values by outputting generated TypeScript interface blocks in the logs. This mechanism flags `(unrecognised)` appliance options or settings, facilitating user reports that allow the maintainer to update the plugin's internal schema for better device support.
